title: Courage, Gleaned
scripture_text: God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love.
reflection: The truth told in love is the hardest, bravest, most loving thing a person can do. It is also the rarest. (The fifth hearing of this day.)
body:
You have been drafting the conversation in your head for weeks. Rehearsing. Walking away. Coming back. The fear of what will happen if you say it is bigger than the cost of not saying it. But the cost of not saying it is also real. The thing is growing, in the silence.

Say it. Say it kindly. Say it clearly. Do not say it all at once. Do not say it in anger. Do not say it as an attack. Say it as a prayer, almost. As a laying down of what is true, in front of the person you love.

The Lord is in the truth. He is also in the love. If you can hold both, you can say the thing.

The conversation is not the end of the relationship. The truth is not the end of the love. It is, often, the beginning.

The fear is not a sign that something is wrong. The fear is a sign that you are doing something brave. The people of the Bible, when they heard the call of God, were mostly afraid. Moses said, "I am slow of speech." Gideon said, "I am the least in my father's house." Jeremiah said, "I am a child." David was a shepherd. The fear is the calling's costume. You put it on when you step into the thing. You take it off later, on the other side.

The Lord does not ask you to be unafraid. He asks you to be brave. Brave is being afraid and stepping anyway. The Lord is the step. The Lord is the road. The Lord is the hand at your back.

The fear does not have to leave before the step. The step and the fear can walk together.

The fear is not a sign that something is wrong. The fear is a sign that you are doing something brave. The people of the Bible, when they heard the call of God, were mostly afraid. Moses said, "I am slow of speech." Gideon said, "I am the least in my father's house." Jeremiah said, "I am a child." David was a shepherd. The fear is the calling's costume. You put it on when you step into the thing. You take it off later, on the other side.

The Lord does not ask you to be unafraid. He asks you to be brave. Brave is being afraid and stepping anyway. The Lord is the step. The Lord is the road. The Lord is the hand at your back.

There is a way of being in the world that does not require you to be ahead, or impressive, or on top of it. The world will not validate this way. The world will tell you to hurry. The Lord is telling you to be still. The Lord is telling you to be here. The Lord is the one who sees you, right now, in this chair, with this breath, with this much strength, with this much doubt. He sees you. He is with you. The seeing is the practice. The being-with is the gift.

The fear does not have to leave before the step. The step and the fear can walk together.

This is the fifth and final time the cycle has come to this day. The harvest is gathered. The hands are tired and the heart is full. The Lord, who began the work in you, will complete it. The cycle is not an ending. It is a Sabbath.
prayer: Lord, give me the words. Give me the courage. Give me the kindness. Be in the room. Amen.
